verdicts.title: Failed trip DELETE silently swallowed — trip vanishes then reappears on next poll with no error
verdicts.reasoning: Verified _delete (core.ts:302-306) only .catch()es, deleteActiveTrip (trip-controls.ts:200-204) and deleteArchivedTrip (handlers.ts:135-139) never inspect res.ok, apiFetch returns Response for 4xx/5xx without throwing, server writes the trip_deletes tombstone + DELETE only inside the committed txn (trips.py:436-450), and pullFromServer full-replaces STATE.trips (api.ts:183) — so a failed delete silently un-removes the trip with no user feedback; not data-loss (nothing destroyed) so P2.

verdicts.title: Failed trip CREATE (daily cap 429 / 500) is silently swallowed; new trip is lost on next poll
verdicts.reasoning: Verified server returns 429 {userCapHit:true} at trips.py:127-133, _upsertWithUpdatedAt returns {ok:false} without throwing on any HTTP error (api.ts:419), the create handler's catch never fires and ignores the return value (trip.ts:227-231), enqueueMutation only runs in apiFetch's network-failure catch (core.ts:251) so a 429/500 is never queued, and pullFromServer full-replaces STATE.trips (api.ts:183) dropping the optimistic trip — but the trip was never persisted so this is a lost-unsaved-draft UX failure, not destruction of saved data, hence P2.

verdicts.title: Cloned trip's marked places keep the SOURCE trip's dayId → wishlist pins become invisible on every day filter
verdicts.reasoning: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['marked_places_json'] verbatim (trips.py:1309) and grep confirms _clean_marked_places is called ONLY on the template path (templates.py:185,220), never in the clone path, while clone generates fresh day ids (trips.py:1393) and NULLs day dates — so cloned to-do pins carry dangling source dayIds that todoMarkers.ts:183 skips on numbered days and todoMarkers.ts:186 skips on Anchor view, making them invisible on the map (they still list on /todo), a real bug at P2.

verdicts.title: No cap on trip date range → unbounded day scaffolding; days beyond 999 silently rejected by the server
verdicts.reasoning: Verified flatpickr is configured with no maxDate/max-span (dateRangePicker.ts:89-101), _scaffoldTripDays loops unbounded (_shared.ts:109), Promise.all(scaffolded.map(upsertDay)) ignores each {ok:false} (trip.ts:229), and upsert_day returns 400 for dayNumber>999 (days.py:72-73) so days 1000+ silently fail while local STATE keeps them — real divergence but only triggered by an extreme >999-day (2.7yr) range, so P3.

verdicts.title: Maps-unavailable country fallback creates the Day-0 anchor at lat/lng 0,0 (Gulf of Guinea)
verdicts.reasoning: The Maps-down fallback builds the picked place with lat:0,lng:0 (_shared.ts:218), the create modal copies these onto the trip (trip.ts:184-185), and the lazy Day-0 creator gates only on typeof activeTrip.lat==='number' (handlers.ts:243-244) — 0 IS a number — so it creates the anchor at 0,0 (handlers.ts:252-253) which todoMarkers.ts:181 treats as 'no pin' only for to-do markers not the anchor; real but cosmetic and only when Maps fails to load, so P3.

verdicts.title: Background /api/data poll replaces STATE.trips while the Edit-Trip modal is open, detaching the edited object
verdicts.reasoning: openEditTripModal takes a STATE.trips reference (invoked with activeTrip at TripBody.tsx:522) and mutates it in place (trip.ts:452-463), while pullFromServer unconditionally rebuilds STATE.trips = allTrips.filter(...) with brand-new objects (api.ts:183) and the modalOpen guard only suppresses the re-navigate at api.ts:335-336 not the STATE replacement — so a mid-edit poll orphans the modal's trip; the edit is still POSTed (no data loss) but causes a render flash and an edge-case stale-stamp 409 only if the trip was also touched server-side, so P2.

verdicts.title: 'Day deleted' success toast is a hardcoded English string (i18n gap)
verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed showLiquidAlert('Day deleted') literal at home-mount/handlers.ts:321 (grep shows it is the sole occurrence), surrounded by t()-localized strings; note the finding's other cited path pages/home/handlers.ts does not exist, but the real file home-mount/handlers.ts has exactly the cited i18n gap, so P3.

verdicts.title: Editing trip dates with all-undated existing days silently no-ops the date change
verdicts.reasoning: Verified oldFirst/oldLast parse numberedDays[0].date/[last].date (trip.ts:527-528) which yield null for empty dates, making startChanged/endChanged both falsy (trip.ts:532-535, they require non-null old dates), so the rebase (539) and extend/shorten (565) blocks are skipped and the new dates land only on trip.dateFrom/dateTo (601-602) never on the day rows; the numberedDays.length===0 scaffold path (518) doesn't apply since dateless numbered days exist — real but narrow edge (existing numbered days all blank-dated), so P3.
